steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Jug found tilling increased wheat yield, then signals 'But some studies of other crops have found the opposite effect.' The correct choice must show no-till outperforming till for a crop other than wheat.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — no-till had a positive effect on rice yield: A different crop (rice) where no-till is better, directly opposing Jug's pro-tilling finding. ✓
  • Choice B — tilling helped soybeans: Same direction as Jug (tilling is better), just a different crop. Not the 'opposite effect.' ✗
  • Choice C — raw yield numbers without a comparison: No effect direction is established, so no 'opposite effect' can be inferred. ✗
  • Choice D — tilling helped wheat: Same crop and same direction as Jug. No contrast at all. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
